user_query:
I’m preparing a small bilingual (English→Spanish) teaching pack that shows how math is used in physics, biology, and economics. Please transform the notes in input/notes/ and generate a structured index so I can demo this in a workshop.

Requirements:
- Update configuration:
  - Edit input/settings.yaml so that:
    - target_languages is set to ["es"].
    - preserve_math is set to true.
  - Keep other fields as-is.
- Implement and run a local script or command that reads input/settings.yaml and processes every .md file under input/notes/ (no external services or Internet APIs). Record the CLI invocation you used in the output manifest.
- Translation rules for each note:
  - Translate only the English prose to Spanish.
  - Preserve LaTeX math exactly as-is (anything inside $...$ or $$...$$) and preserve fenced code blocks ```...``` unchanged.
  - Use input/glossary.csv to enforce consistent terminology: replace occurrences of the English terms with the exact Spanish pair, matching whole words/phrases case-insensitively, but never inside LaTeX math or code fences.
  - Copy the YAML front matter from the source and add two keys: lang: es and translated_from: en.
  - Save each translated file to output/es/<original_basename>.md (e.g., physics.md → output/es/physics.md).
- Structured outputs for verification:
  - output/index.json: an array where each item summarizes one source note with fields:
    - file: source file path relative to workspace (e.g., input/notes/physics.md)
    - title: from front matter
    - field: from front matter
    - math_topics: array from front matter
    - keywords: array from front matter
    - equation_count: number of inline math segments ($...$ or $$...$$) in the body
    - word_count_en: word count of the English body excluding math and code
    - word_count_es: word count of the Spanish body excluding math and code
  - output/glossary_coverage.csv: header = file,english_term,spanish_term,count_in_source,count_in_translation; one row per (note, term) where the English term appears in the source body (outside math/code). Count occurrences in the source and verify the Spanish term appears that many times in the translated body (outside math/code).
  - output/manifest.json: include project_name (from settings.yaml), languages_processed, source_files (count), total_equations (sum of equation_count), glossary_terms_applied_total (sum of count_in_translation across all rows), and cli_invocation (the command you ran).

Inputs to use:
- input/settings.yaml (to be updated as above)
- input/glossary.csv (term mappings to enforce)
- input/notes/*.md (3 notes with YAML front matter and body text)

Deliverables to create:
- Translated notes under output/es/ with preserved math and code and updated front matter.
- output/index.json summarizing each note as specified.
- output/glossary_coverage.csv with coverage counts.
- output/manifest.json with the required metadata, including the exact CLI invocation you used.

Please keep the workflow local and reproducible: parse the YAML front matter and bodies from the provided files, use the given glossary, update input/settings.yaml as specified, and run your script/command to generate all outputs. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
